{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether self-efficacy mediates the relationship between transformational leadership behaviours and healthcare workers' sleep quality. Reviewing the provided documents, there is no direct mention of self-efficacy as a mediator in any relationship involving transformational leadership and sleep quality. While Document [10] discusses self-efficacy in the context of work competence and belief in one's ability to perform work activities, it does not link this to transformational leadership or sleep quality. Transformational leadership is discussed in several documents (e.g., Document [2], [21]), highlighting its role in building trust, fostering relationships, and promoting a positive work culture. Document [6] and [30] emphasize that employee health, sleep quality, and work culture are influenced by leadership and work environment, but no explicit mediating role of self-efficacy is described. There is no evidence in the documents that self-efficacy acts as a mediator between transformational leadership and sleep quality.
